reason: Both answers correctly diagnose the Goodhart's law dynamics (bonus attracted low-intent referrals), note absolute activated users are roughly flat while cost-per-signup misleads, prescribe paying on activation, and recommend segmenting referred vs organic before concluding, plus flag fraud risk. Substantively nearly identical. The difference is B opens with a redundant "Sketch" section restating bullets the main answer repeats almost verbatim, adding length without new insight, while A goes straight to the point more concisely. Per the rubric's caution against rewarding padding, A is marginally better.
